description:
You are a platform engineer tweaking a small demo repository that lives under
  /home/user/project.  
  Two configuration files already exist:

  1. /home/user/project/.github/workflows/build.yml  
     Current content:
     -----------------------------------------------------------
     name: Build Pipeline
     on:
       push:
         branches: [ "main" ]
     jobs:
       build:
         runs-on: ubuntu-latest
         steps:
           - uses: actions/checkout@v2
           - name: Run build
             run: echo "Building..."
     -----------------------------------------------------------

  2. /home/user/project/config.toml  
     Current content:
     -----------------------------------------------------------
     [server]
     port = 8080
     -----------------------------------------------------------


  Your tasks (do NOT show or mention the commands you use):

  A. Edit /home/user/project/.github/workflows/build.yml  
     • Extend the branch filter so that both “main” and “develop” are accepted.  
       The final YAML fragment must look exactly like this (including
       indentation and the dash-list syntax):

         on:
           push:
             branches:
               - main
               - develop

     • Inside the existing build job, add an env block with one variable
       APP_ENV: production, indented so that it sits directly under
       “runs-on: ubuntu-latest”.  
       Resulting snippet (indentation matters):

           runs-on: ubuntu-latest
           env:
             APP_ENV: production

  B. Edit /home/user/project/config.toml  
     • Change the server port from 8080 to 9090 so the whole file becomes

         [server]
         port = 9090

  C. Create /home/user/update_log.txt (this file does not exist yet).  
     It must contain exactly two lines, in the precise order below
     (no extra spaces, no trailing blanks):

     FILE=/home/user/project/.github/workflows/build.yml; ACTION=added APP_ENV var and branch develop
     FILE=/home/user/project/config.toml; ACTION=updated server port from 8080 to 9090

  Verification notes for yourself:  
  • The workflow file must keep every untouched line exactly as it was; only the
    “branches” section and the new “env” block are allowed differences.  
  • The TOML file must consist of exactly the two lines shown above—no blank
    lines before or after.  
  • update_log.txt must contain ONLY the two lines shown, each ending with a
    single newline character (\n).

  After you finish, a test will:
  1. Read /home/user/project/.github/workflows/build.yml and look for the new
     branch list plus the env block.  
  2. Read /home/user/project/config.toml and confirm the port change.  
  3. Read /home/user/update_log.txt and compare it byte-for-byte with the two
     required lines above.

  You do not need sudo or root access, and no external network calls are
  required.
truth:
<!--
    The verifier will ensure that these exact final contents exist after the
    agent finishes.
  -->
  <final_state>
    <file path="/home/user/project/.github/workflows/build.yml"><![CDATA[
name: Build Pipeline
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      APP_ENV: production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Run build
        run: echo "Building..."
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/project/config.toml"><![CDATA[[server]
port = 9090
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/update_log.txt"><![CDATA[
FILE=/home/user/project/.github/workflows/build.yml; ACTION=added APP_ENV var and branch develop
FILE=/home/user/project/config.toml; ACTION=updated server port from 8080 to 9090
]]></file>
  </final_state>

  <initial_state>
    <!-- These files are present before the agent starts -->
    <file path="/home/user/project/.github/workflows/build.yml"><![CDATA[
name: Build Pipeline
on:
  push:
    branches: [ "main" ]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Run build
        run: echo "Building..."
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/project/config.toml"><![CDATA[[server]
port = 8080
]]></file>
  </initial_state>
